mechanism_narrative: A sharp downward move in the federal funds rate of at least 1.5 percentage points within one month signals aggressive monetary easing that compresses discount rates on equity cash flows, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS drops by more than 1.5 percentage points from prior month
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: The level of the unemployment rate inversely predicts near-term equity returns: higher unemployment indicates reduced consumer spending and corporate earnings, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days, regardless of regime.
failure_condition: Spearman rank correlation between UNRATE level and 20-day forward returns is not negative at the 90% confidence level over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When both retail sales and housing starts are above their respective 6-month moving averages, it signals robust aggregate demand that boosts corporate profits and leads to positive S&P 500 forward returns over a quarterly horizon.
candidate_trigger: Both RSAFS 3-month average and HOUST 3-month average exceed their respective 6-month moving averages
failure_condition: In triggered instances, the average 60-day return is not positive at the 5% significance level, or the signal fails more than 40% of the time in historical backtests.

mechanism_narrative: During a supply disruption regime defined by a sharp month-over-month decline in industrial production, further declines signal worsening supply bottlenecks and reduced output, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: After trigger activation, median 20-day S&P 500 return is greater than zero, or the negative signal is correct less than 55% of the time.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in headline CPI exceeding 0.5% signals rising inflation that prompts expectations of monetary tightening, raising discount rates and depressing equity valuations over the subsequent 20 trading days.
failure_condition: After trigger, the 20-day S&P 500 forward return distribution is not statistically different from zero, or the signal yields a negative Sharpe ratio in out-of-sample periods.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly real GDP growth is a fundamental driver of corporate earnings; a rising GDPC1 level relative to the prior quarter signals expanding economic activity and predicts positive S&P 500 returns over a quarterly horizon, irrespective of regime.
failure_condition: The correlation between quarterly GDP growth and subsequent 60-day S&P 500 returns is not positive at the 90% confidence level over any 5-year window.

mechanism_narrative: The joint condition of elevated unemployment (UNRATE above 5%) and very low policy rates (FEDFUNDS below 2%) signals a deeply accommodative monetary stance in a weak economy, anticipating a recovery that produces positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: In triggered instances over a 10-year backtest, the average 20-day forward return is not positive or the signal's hit rate is below 55%.

mechanism_narrative: During a recessionary regime confirmed by two consecutive monthly declines in nonfarm payrolls, further drops in PAYEMS indicate accelerating labor market deterioration that drives down consumer demand and corporate profits, resulting in negative S&P 500 returns over the subsequent week.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS monthly change negative for two consecutive months
failure_condition: After trigger, the 5-day forward S&P 500 return is positive more than 40% of the time or the mean signal return is not significantly below zero (t-test p > 0.10).
